page: Amazon Web Services r6in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance offers 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features a dedicated CPU allocation running at 3.5 GHz, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the r6in.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores. Its memory subsystem shows high throughput, with read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 313.67 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 136.45 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 10% tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 25%, processing 86.05 million operations per second. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks remain in the average tier.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive and EBS-reliant workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial network throughput offer a balanced resource density for enterprise-scale deployments without the overhead of local storage. This profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale relational databases, in-memory caches like Redis, and high-throughput data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance is highly optimized for networking, offering a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps. In benchmarks, it exhibits strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic execution, with Passmark encryption testing placing in the top 10%. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also highly competitive, scoring in the top 25%. This hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and high-throughput network data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, memory-optimized virtual server featuring 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores of Intel Xeon 8375C processors.
Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read/write bandwidth.
Features 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network traffic.
Delivers top-tier cryptographic performance and strong database operation speeds.
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
